question: What is overfitting?
What is the difference between training and testing data?
What is the difference between classification and regression? Give a real-world example of each.
What is the difference between training accuracy and validation accuracy, and why does the gap between them matter?
What is overfitting and what are two simple techniques you can use to reduce it?
Why is feature scaling important, and when would you use standardization versus normalization?
Explain how a decision tree makes predictions and what criteria it uses to split nodes.
Explain the difference between bagging and boosting. When would you choose Random Forest over XGBoost?
Compare grid search, random search, and Bayesian optimization for hyperparameter tuning. What are the trade-offs of each?
You have a dataset with 95% negative and 5% positive samples. Walk me through your strategy to handle this class imbalance.
When is AUC-ROC a misleading metric and what would you use instead? Provide a concrete scenario.
Explain PCA intuitively and describe a production scenario where you would and would not use it.
How do you design a machine learning system that must make real-time predictions under 50ms latency constraints while maintaining high accuracy?
Explain the difference between correlation and causation in the context of ML. How would you design an experiment to establish causal relationships in a production ML system?
Your model's performance degrades 3 months after deployment. Describe your systematic approach to diagnosing and resolving distribution shift.
How would you audit a credit scoring model for demographic bias and what mitigation strategies would you implement without significantly sacrificing accuracy?
Design a multi-armed bandit system for real-time content personalization. How do you balance exploration vs. exploitation at scale?
What is backpropagation and why is it essential for training neural networks?
What is the vanishing gradient problem and how does ReLU help address it compared to sigmoid?
What is dropout and at what phase of training and inference do you apply it?
What is the role of the learning rate in training a neural network and what happens when it is too high or too low?
What is a convolutional layer and why is it more suitable for image data than a fully connected layer?
Describe the process of fine-tuning a pretrained model. When would you freeze layers versus train the entire network?
Compare Adam, SGD with momentum, and RMSProp. In what scenarios would you prefer each optimizer?
Explain the architecture of an LSTM and how it solves the long-term dependency problem compared to a vanilla RNN.
How does batch normalization work and what problems does it solve? What are its limitations?
When would you use focal loss instead of binary cross-entropy? Implement the formula and explain each component.
Walk me through designing a custom neural network architecture for a multimodal task combining text and images. What are the key considerations?
Explain the differences between data parallelism and model parallelism for distributed training. When would you use each?
Compare quantization, pruning, and knowledge distillation for model compression. Design a compression pipeline for deploying a large model on edge devices.
Derive the scaled dot-product attention mechanism. Why is the scaling factor of sqrt(d_k) necessary and what happens without it?
Your deep learning model's loss is not decreasing after 10 epochs. Describe a systematic debugging checklist you would follow.
What is the difference between a list and a tuple in Python? When would you prefer one over the other in a data processing pipeline?
What are the main differences between NumPy arrays and Python lists? Why are NumPy arrays preferred for numerical computation?
How do you handle missing values in a Pandas DataFrame? Describe at least three methods.
Explain list comprehensions and generator expressions. When is a generator more appropriate than a list?
What is the purpose of __init__ and __repr__ in a Python class? Write a simple example class for a ML Dataset object.
You have a Python data preprocessing pipeline that is too slow. Walk me through profiling it and at least three strategies to optimize performance.
Explain the difference between threading, multiprocessing, and asyncio in Python. Which would you use for a CPU-bound ML data pipeline and why?
Write a Python decorator that logs the execution time of any function. How would you extend this for monitoring in a production ML system?
How would you write unit tests for a feature engineering function using pytest? What edge cases would you cover?
You need to process a 100GB CSV file in Python on a machine with 16GB RAM. What approaches would you use?
How would you design a reusable, production-grade Python ML library? Describe your approach to packaging, versioning, dependency management, and backward compatibility.
Explain how Python's GIL affects ML workloads and how tools like Cython, ctypes, or Numba can be used to bypass it.
Explain how you would implement a plugin-based architecture in Python to allow interchangeable ML model backends. Which design pattern would you use?
Write a custom PyTorch Dataset and DataLoader for a multi-label classification task with dynamic augmentation applied only during training.
What are metaclasses in Python and give a practical AI engineering scenario where using one would be justified over simpler alternatives.
What is the difference between probability and likelihood? Provide an example in the context of a machine learning model.
What is the difference between mean, median, and mode? In a dataset with many outliers, which is most appropriate to report?
What is a normal distribution and why is it so commonly assumed in ML models? Give an example where this assumption breaks down.
What is a p-value and what does it mean when a result is statistically significant at p < 0.05?
What is the difference between Pearson and Spearman correlation? When would you use each?
Explain Bayes' theorem and how it is used in a Naive Bayes classifier. What is the 'naive' assumption and when does it fail?
Walk me through designing a statistically valid A/B test for a new ML-powered recommendation feature. How do you determine sample size?
What is a confidence interval and how would you use bootstrapping to compute one for a model's F1 score?
When would you use a t-test versus a Mann-Whitney U test? Apply this reasoning to evaluating two model versions.
Explain Maximum Likelihood Estimation. How does it relate to minimizing cross-entropy loss in a classification model?
Explain the difference between observational studies and randomized controlled trials in the context of ML evaluation. How do propensity score matching help?
Compare frequentist and Bayesian approaches to model evaluation. When would you advocate for a Bayesian A/B test over a frequentist one in a production system?
How would you detect and handle multiple testing problems when running simultaneous A/B tests across many model features?
Explain how survival analysis can be applied to a customer churn prediction problem. What are the advantages over standard binary classification?
Explain KL divergence and its role in variational autoencoders. How does it differ from Jensen-Shannon divergence and when would you prefer each?
What is ETL and how does it differ from ELT? Give an example of when you would use each in an ML context.
Write a SQL query to find the top 5 customers by total purchase amount, along with the number of purchases they made.
What are the differences between CSV, JSON, Parquet, and Avro? Which would you use for storing large ML training datasets and why?
What are the most common data quality issues you would check for before training a machine learning model?
What is the difference between a relational database and a NoSQL database? When would you use a NoSQL database in an ML project?
Compare Apache Kafka and Apache Spark Streaming for real-time ML feature generation. When would you use each?
What is a feature store and what problems does it solve in a large-scale ML system? Describe a scenario where it provides clear value.
How would you version datasets for reproducible ML experiments? Describe the tools and strategies you would use.
How would you design an Apache Airflow DAG for a daily ML training pipeline? What are the key failure modes and how would you handle them?
Explain the concept of a data lakehouse. How does Delta Lake or Apache Iceberg improve upon a traditional data lake for ML workflows?
Design a scalable data ingestion and feature engineering platform that serves both batch training and real-time inference for 10 million daily users.
How would you implement data lineage tracking and governance in an ML platform to comply with GDPR, including the right to be forgotten?
Your Spark job processing 10TB of training data takes 6 hours. Walk me through diagnosing the bottleneck and optimizing it.
Compare Lambda and Kappa architectures for an ML system requiring both historical batch retraining and real-time feature updates. Which would you choose and why?
Compare Pinecone, Weaviate, and pgvector for storing and retrieving embeddings at scale. What are the trade-offs for a production RAG system?
What is experiment tracking and why is it important? What information would you log for each training run?
What is the difference between batch inference and real-time inference? Give an example use case for each.
What is Docker and why is it useful for deploying ML models? What would you include in a Dockerfile for a Python ML application?
How do you version control machine learning models and why is model versioning different from code versioning?
What is data drift and how would you detect it in a production ML system?
Design a CI/CD pipeline for an ML model. What validation steps would you include before promoting a model to production?
What is an ML model registry and how does it integrate with the model deployment lifecycle? Describe a workflow using MLflow.
Compare blue-green deployment, canary releases, and shadow mode for deploying a new ML model. Which would you choose for a high-risk fraud detection model?
Explain the trade-offs between deploying ML models on Kubernetes versus serverless platforms like AWS Lambda.
How would you design an automated model retraining pipeline that triggers based on data drift detection rather than a fixed schedule?
Design a self-service ML platform for 200 data scientists. What components would you build and what open-source tools would you integrate?
What is the difference between model monitoring and model observability? Design a comprehensive observability stack for a production ML system.
Your ML inference infrastructure costs $500K per month. Walk me through a systematic approach to reduce costs by 40% without degrading user experience.
How would you implement model governance including audit trails, approval workflows, and compliance reporting in a regulated financial services company?
How would you design an ML system with 99.9% uptime SLA? Describe your fallback strategies when the primary model is unavailable.
What is a transformer architecture and what makes it different from RNNs for processing text?
What is tokenization and why does the choice of tokenizer affect LLM performance? What is byte-pair encoding?
What is prompt engineering? Give examples of zero-shot, one-shot, and few-shot prompting.
How would you call the OpenAI API to summarize a document? What parameters like temperature and max_tokens do and why do they matter?
What is a RAG system? Explain the basic flow of how it works and what problem it solves.
Compare full fine-tuning, LoRA, and QLoRA for adapting an LLM to a domain-specific task. What are the trade-offs in compute, data, and performance?
Design a production RAG pipeline for a customer support bot. How would you handle chunking strategy, embedding selection, and retrieval ranking?
How would you evaluate the quality of an LLM-based application? Describe both automated and human evaluation approaches.
What causes LLM hallucinations and what techniques can you implement at inference time to reduce them in a production system?
Explain the ReAct pattern for LLM agents. How would you implement tool use with error handling in a production agentic system?
Explain how grouped query attention and sliding window attention improve LLM inference efficiency compared to standard multi-head attention.
Explain the RLHF training pipeline including supervised fine-tuning, reward model training, and PPO optimization. What are the key failure modes?
Explain KV caching, speculative decoding, and continuous batching for LLM inference. How would you select between vLLM, TensorRT-LLM, and TGI for a production deployment?
Design a multi-agent LLM system for automated software engineering that can plan, code, test, and debug. How do you manage state, context limits, and reliability?
How would you implement a robust guardrail system for an enterprise LLM application handling sensitive healthcare data, including input filtering, output validation, and PII detection?
What is the difference between RGB and grayscale images? How are images represented as tensors in a deep learning framework?
What is data augmentation and why is it important for training CV models? Give five examples of common image augmentation techniques.
What is ImageNet and why is pretraining on it useful? Name three commonly used pretrained CNN architectures.
What is the difference between image classification, object detection, and semantic segmentation?
What is Intersection over Union (IoU) and how is it used to evaluate object detection models?
Compare ResNet, EfficientNet, and Vision Transformer (ViT) architectures. When would you choose each for a production CV task?
Explain how YOLO works compared to a two-stage detector like Faster R-CNN. What are the trade-offs in speed and accuracy?
What is the difference between semantic segmentation, instance segmentation, and panoptic segmentation? Name a real-world application for each.
You need to train a defect detection model but only have 200 labeled images. What techniques would you use to build a production-quality model?
How would you approach action recognition in video data? Compare 3D CNN and two-stream network approaches.
Explain how CLIP and SAM (Segment Anything Model) work. Design a zero-shot product defect detection system using foundation models.
Design an end-to-end real-time CV system for autonomous quality control on a manufacturing line processing 60 frames per second. Cover hardware, model, and pipeline choices.
Explain how contrastive learning methods like SimCLR and MoCo work. How would you use self-supervised pretraining to improve a medical imaging model with limited labels?
Compare NeRF and Gaussian Splatting for 3D scene reconstruction. What are practical applications and limitations of each in an enterprise setting?
Design a visual question answering system that must handle out-of-distribution queries gracefully. How do you measure and improve its reliability in production?
